text: When you write code, bugs are inevitable. The first step in fixing them is identifying what went wrong—this could be a typo, a logic error, or an unexpected input. A systematic approach is essential: reproduce the problem, isolate the cause, and test your fix. Useful debugging tools include breakpoints to pause execution, step-through features to watch variable changes, and error messages that point to the issue. Another powerful technique is logging—adding print statements or using a logger to track program flow. By staying calm and following these steps, you can solve most bugs efficiently.
